intro: Arctium lappa gum, also known as burdock root gum, is a natural adhesive derived from the roots of the burdock plant. This gum has been used for centuries in traditional medicine and as a sustainable alternative to synthetic adhesives. Its unique properties make it a popular choice for crafting, woodworking, and other applications where a non-toxic, biodegradable adhesive is desired.

recipe: To make arctium lappa gum for medicinal use, harvest the roots of the plant, clean and chop them into small pieces, and then boil them in water to create a liquid extract.
